svg.text: Giant Eagle Gold Layer: Lift-and-Shift vs Lakehouse-Native
All 51 Snowflake views covered. Same Power BI consumes either side via compatibility view layer.
Mechanical port. Inherits the per-DC duplication and per-BU view sprawl.

g.text: Transactions / receiving / outbound / shipment / orders / tasks:
pix_entry · rcv_asn(_line) · rcv_purchase_order(_line, _status) · ppk_olpn(_detail) · tsk_task · dco_order · shp_shipment · etc.

svg.text: GOLD LIFT-AND-SHIFT (51 Delta views, grouped by BU)

g.text: Each BU = same template, different facility filter.
Six near-identical copies of three patterns.
Heterogeneous shapes; no obvious pattern reuse.
of just 3 templates: IROO, QROO, SNAPSHOT.
Schema changes ripple through N copies.
Per-DC SQL maintenance is ongoing tax.

svg.text: Version B: Lakehouse-Native (de facto Gold by Databricks expert)
Conformed dimensions + 12 facts + 51 compatibility views (template + filter).

g.text: Same Bronze feeds both versions. Lakeflow Connect-managed; no duplication.
BICEPS, HBC, ASF, BRM, FFM, OKG, OKP, CCS

svg.text: CONFORMED FACTS (12 tables, liquid-clustered on facility + date)
COMPATIBILITY VIEW LAYER (51 views, one per Snowflake view shape)

g.text: Same column shapes as Version A. Power BI sees no difference.
Template-then-filter pattern (collapses ~40 of 51 views to ~6 templates):
v_ccs_item_snapshot → v_hbc_item_snapshot_d0080, v_asf, v_brm, v_ffm, v_okg, v_okp
misha_test_v: confirmed dev artifact, intentionally not migrated. Retire at cutover.
Decision input: A is low-risk and mechanical. B is the expert opinionated remodel, collapses ~40 views to ~6 templates, adds clustering, simpler maintenance.
Both expose identical Power BI surfaces via the compatibility layer. Same query, same result, different engine room.
Repoints to either Gold version with identical column shapes
